title: NOC 72105 – Ironworkers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72105 professionals are the structural specialists who build the "skeletons" of Canada’s most iconic structures. As a TEER 2 occupation, Ironworkers fabricate, erect, hoist, and install structural steel, reinforcing bars (rebar), and ornamental iron for buildings, bridges, and dams. In 2026, as Canada scales up "Vertical Urbanization" and major transit infrastructure, Ironworkers are in high demand to support high-density residential towers and large-scale industrial builds. For immigration, this role is a priority for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and the 2026 Express Entry Trade Occupations Category.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group721 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesMinor Group7210 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesNOC Code72105

duties: Blueprint Reading: Review blueprints and specifications to determine layout and the sequence of hoisting and installation.
Hoisting & Rigging: Erect and install scaffolding, hoisting equipment, and rigging to move heavy steel units into position.
Structural Assembly: Align, bolt, or weld structural steel units in place according to engineering drawings.
Reinforcing: Position and secure steel bars (rebar) or metal mesh in concrete forms to reinforce structures like highways and building foundations.
Ornamental Work: Install architectural metalwork, including curtain walls, metal stairways, railings, and power doors.
Inspection: Examine structures and equipment for defects, deterioration, or non-compliance with building codes.
Dismantling: May dismantle older structures and equipment as part of redevelopment or industrial decommissioning.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a 2-to-3-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over 3 years of work experience in the trade plus college-level courses.

Certification (Trade Seal):Trade certification is compulsory in Alberta (for Structural/Ornamental and Metal Building Systems).Trade certification is available (but voluntary) in all other provinces and territories.Obtaining the Red Seal Endorsement is highly recommended for interprovincial mobility and is a major asset for PR applications.
Trade certification is compulsory in Alberta (for Structural/Ornamental and Metal Building Systems).
Trade certification is available (but voluntary) in all other provinces and territories.
Obtaining the Red Seal Endorsement is highly recommended for interprovincial mobility and is a major asset for PR applications.
Physical stamina, comfort with heights, and excellent spatial awareness are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: Senior Ironworkers and those working on specialized "rebar" or high-rise structural contracts in 2026 often earn annual salaries between $85,000 and $110,000 inclusive of overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), while growth is moderate, the sector faces a significant replacement demand as senior "Bridge and Structural" specialists retire. The shift toward Pre-engineered Metal Buildings and modular construction ensures a consistent need for erectors who can work with modern, lightweight materials.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Ironworkers is Moderate to Good, with higher concentrations in urban expansion zones. In 2026, Ontario (GTA), British Columbia, and Alberta are the primary hiring hubs. The demand is driven by massive infrastructure investments and new multi-unit housing developments. While the sector is balanced in some regions, the shortage of Reinforcing Rodworkers and Structural Steel Erectors for complex bridge and dam projects remains a critical pressure point for Canadian contractors.
Signature Infrastructure Projects: Work on high-profile projects like the Gordie Howe International Bridge or Canada’s expanding subway and LRT networks.
Specialized High Wages: Ironworking offers high earning potential, especially for those specialized in high-rise structural assembly or heavy industrial rigging.
Safety-First Culture: Canada’s strict Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) standards ensure that Ironworkers operate with the best fall-protection and hoisting technology.
Strategic PR Pathway: As a "Trade Occupation," Ironworkers are eligible for targeted Express Entry draws, which often feature lower CRS requirements than general draws.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): The primary route for tradespeople with at least 2 years of work experience.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: A fast-track route for Ironworkers supporting the province’s massive residential and transportation infrastructure.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Priority for Ironworkers currently employed in the province’s compulsory-certified trade environments.
